ucs.title: [LLM] APT33 / Peach Sandstorm password-spray → successful Entra ID sign-in → new device registration
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Peach Sandstorm (HOLMIUM / Refined Kitten) 2023-2024 intrusion chain documented by Microsoft: low-and-slow password spraying against Entra ID at aerospace/defense/energy targets, followed by a single successful authentication from the spraying infrastructure, followed within hours by attacker-driven Azure AD device registration to obtain a Primary Refresh Token for persistence. Correlates AAD spray pattern → successful logon → identity-directory device registration on the same UPN.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count, dc(Authentication.user) as DistinctUsers, values(Authentication.user) as SprayedUsers, count(eval('Authentication.action'=="failure")) as Failures, count(eval('Authentication.action'=="success")) as Successes from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.app="AzureActiveDirectory" earliest=-24h by Authentication.src, Authentication.signature_id | where DistinctUsers>=15 AND Failures>=20 AND Successes>=1 | rename Authentication.src as src | join type=inner src [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Authentication.user) as CompromisedUser, min(_time) as FirstSuccess from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.app="AzureActiveDirectory" Authentication.action="success" earliest=-24h by Authentication.src ] | join type=inner CompromisedUser [| tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as DeviceRegistrationTime, values(Change.object) as RegisteredDevice from datamodel=Change where Change.action="created" Change.object_category="device" (Change.change_type="AAD" OR Change.change_type="AzureAD") earliest=-24h by Change.user | rename Change.user as CompromisedUser ] | where DeviceRegistrationTime>FirstSuccess AND (DeviceRegistrationTime-FirstSuccess)<14400 | table src, DistinctUsers, Failures, Successes, CompromisedUser, FirstSuccess, DeviceRegistrationTime, RegisteredDevice
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let SprayWindow = 1h;
let RegoWindow = 4h;
// 1. Spraying source IPs — many distinct users, mostly failed, at least one success (the foothold)
let SprayIPs =
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | summarize
        DistinctUsers = dcount(AccountUpn),
        Fails = countif(ErrorCode != 0),
        Success = countif(ErrorCode == 0),
        SuccessUsers = make_set_if(AccountUpn, ErrorCode == 0, 50),
        FirstSuccess = minif(Timestamp, ErrorCode == 0)
        by IPAddress, bin(Timestamp, SprayWindow)
    | where DistinctUsers >= 15 and Fails >= 20 and Success >= 1   // 15/20/1 = empirical Peach Sandstorm shape
    | mv-expand SuccessUsers to typeof(string)
    | project IPAddress, FirstSuccess, CompromisedUpn = SuccessUsers, DistinctUsers, Fails;
// 2. Same UPN registers a device shortly after the successful spray hit
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActionType has_any ("Device Registration", "Registered device", "Add registered owner", "Add registered users to device")
| project RegoTime = Timestamp, RegoActor = AccountUpn, TargetDevice = TargetAccountDisplayName, RegoIP = IPAddress, RegoFields = AdditionalFields
| join kind=inner SprayIPs on $left.RegoActor == $right.CompromisedUpn
| where RegoTime between (FirstSuccess .. FirstSuccess + RegoWindow)
| project FirstSuccess, RegoTime,
          MinutesAfterSpray = datetime_diff('minute', RegoTime, FirstSuccess),
          SprayIP = IPAddress, RegoIP, CompromisedUpn, TargetDevice,
          DistinctUsers, Fails, RegoFields
| order by FirstSuccess desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let SprayWindow = 1h;
let RegoWindow = 4h;
// 1. Password-spray source IPs (many users sprayed, ≥1 success)
let SprayIPs =
    SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | summarize
        DistinctUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
        Fails = countif(ResultType != 0),
        Success = countif(ResultType == 0),
        SuccessUsers = make_set_if(UserPrincipalName, ResultType == 0, 50),
        FirstSuccess = minif(TimeGenerated, ResultType == 0)
        by IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, SprayWindow)
    | where DistinctUsers >= 15 and Fails >= 20 and Success >= 1
    | mv-expand SuccessUsers to typeof(string)
    | project IPAddress, FirstSuccess, CompromisedUpn = SuccessUsers, DistinctUsers, Fails;
// 2. New device registration / join via AuditLogs for the same UPN
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where Category =~ "Device" or LoggedByService =~ "Core Directory"
| where OperationName has_any ("Add device", "Add registered owner", "Add registered users to device", "Register device")
| extend RegoActorUpn = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName)
| extend RegoActorIP  = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress)
| extend TargetDevice = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| project RegoTime = TimeGenerated, RegoActorUpn, RegoActorIP, TargetDevice, OperationName
| join kind=inner SprayIPs on $left.RegoActorUpn == $right.CompromisedUpn
| where RegoTime between (FirstSuccess .. FirstSuccess + RegoWindow)
| project FirstSuccess, RegoTime,
          MinutesAfterSpray = datetime_diff('minute', RegoTime, FirstSuccess),
          SprayIP = IPAddress, RegoActorIP, CompromisedUpn = RegoActorUpn,
          TargetDevice, OperationName, DistinctUsers, Fails
| order by FirstSuccess desc
ucs.rationale: This is the documented Peach Sandstorm (Microsoft / Mandiant Sept-2023 advisory + 2024 FalseFont follow-on) entry pattern, not a generic spray detector. The combination — high distinct-user spray + at least one success from the same IP + AAD device-registration on the freshly compromised UPN within 4 hours — is uniquely APT33: they specifically pivot from sprayed credentials to device-join in orde

ucs.title: [LLM] APT33 hands-on-keyboard: AnyDesk / Plink deployment after Entra ID foothold (Tickler / FalseFont staging)
ucs.description: Catches the post-spray, hands-on-keyboard stage of APT33 / Peach Sandstorm intrusions where the actor sideloads remote-access tooling (AnyDesk, Plink/PuTTY tunnel, Eldorado / Tickler dropper) onto a freshly accessed host and immediately establishes outbound to anydesk[.]com or attacker-controlled cloud storage (Azure Blob / Backblaze) — the documented Microsoft Tickler / FalseFont 2023-2024 tradecraft for satellite, defense and pharma targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as FirstSeen, values(Processes.process) as CmdLines, values(Processes.parent_process_name) as Parents, values(Processes.user) as Users from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("AnyDesk.exe","plink.exe","putty.exe","ngrok.exe")) AND Processes.user!="SYSTEM" AND Processes.user!="*$" earliest=-7d by Processes.dest, Processes.process_name | rename Processes.dest as dest Processes.process_name as process_name | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t values(All_Traffic.dest) as RemoteIP, values(All_Traffic.dest_host) as RemoteHost from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_host IN ("*.net.anydesk.com","*.anydesk.com","relay-*.net.anydesk.com") OR All_Traffic.dest_port IN (7070,80,443)) earliest=-7d by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as dest ] | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Authentication.src) as SignInIP from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.app="AzureActiveDirectory" Authentication.action="success" earliest=-7d by Authentication.dest | rename Authentication.dest as dest ] | table FirstSeen, dest, Users, process_name, CmdLines, Parents, RemoteHost, SignInIP
ucs.kql:
let Lookback = 7d;
let RatBins = dynamic(["anydesk.exe","plink.exe","putty.exe","ngrok.exe","chisel.exe","frpc.exe","revsocks.exe"]);
let RatProc =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$" and AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
    | where FileName in~ (RatBins)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","setup.exe","explorer.exe")        // suppress legit installs
            or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\")
    | project ProcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountUpn, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath;
let RatNet =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where (RemoteUrl has "anydesk.com" or RemoteUrl has "ngrok.io" or RemoteUrl has "trycloudflare.com")
         or (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (RatBins) and RemoteIPType == "Public")
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, NetProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName;
RatProc
| join kind=inner RatNet on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (ProcTime - 5m .. ProcTime + 30m)
// 3. Pivot — the same device's logged-on user had a recent risky/MFA-failed/foreign Entra sign-in
| join kind=leftouter (
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("medium","high") or IsAnonymousProxy == true
    | project SignInTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, SignInIP = IPAddress, Country, RiskLevelDuringSignIn
  ) on AccountUpn
| where isempty(SignInTime) or SignInTime between (ProcTime - 24h .. ProcTime)
| project ProcTime, DeviceName, AccountUpn, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, SignInTime, SignInIP, Country, RiskLevelDuringSignIn
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let Lookback = 7d;
let RatBins = dynamic(["anydesk.exe","plink.exe","putty.exe","ngrok.exe","chisel.exe","frpc.exe","revsocks.exe"]);
let RatProc =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ChildExe = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
    | where ChildExe in (RatBins)
    | where (ParentProcessName !has @"\msiexec.exe" and ParentProcessName !has @"\setup.exe")
         or NewProcessName has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\")
    | project ProcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ChildExe, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
let RatNet =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where DestinationHostName has_any ("anydesk.com", "ngrok.io", "trycloudflare.com")
         or RequestURL       has_any ("anydesk.com", "ngrok.io", "trycloudflare.com")
    | project NetTime = TimeGenerated, Computer = SourceHostName, RemoteIP = DestinationIP, RemoteUrl = coalesce(DestinationHostName, RequestURL);
RatProc
| join kind=inner RatNet on Computer
| where NetTime between (ProcTime - 5m .. ProcTime + 30m)
| join kind=leftouter (
    SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where ResultType == 0
    | where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("medium","high")
    | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(tostring(LocationDetails)).countryOrRegion)
    | project SignInTime = TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, SignInIP = IPAddress, Country, RiskLevelDuringSignIn
  ) on $left.Account == $right.UserPrincipalName
| where isempty(SignInTime) or SignInTime between (ProcTime - 24h .. ProcTime)
| project ProcTime, Computer, Account, ChildExe, CommandLine, ParentProcessName,
          RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, SignInTime, SignInIP, Country, RiskLevelDuringSignIn
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT33 / Peach Sandstorm AnyDesk-or-Plink Deployment From User-Writable Path
id: 9b3a64a4-a14e-4d7a-9bda-ap33-anydesk
status: experimental
description: Detects AnyDesk, Plink/PuTTY, ngrok or chisel launching out of AppData / Temp / Public / ProgramData by a non-system user account — APT33 / Tickler tradecraft for hands-on-keyboard remote access after Entra ID compromise.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2023/09/14/peach-sandstorm-password-spray-campaigns-enable-intelligence-collection/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2024/08/28/peach-sandstorm-deploys-new-custom-tickler-malware/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.t1105
  - g0064
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\AnyDesk.exe'
      - '\plink.exe'
      - '\putty.exe'
      - '\ngrok.exe'
      - '\chisel.exe'
      - '\frpc.exe'
      - '\revsocks.exe'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\Public\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_image and selection_path and not filter_machine_account
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate sysadmin use of PuTTY/Plink from a desktop AppData install
  - Authorised AnyDesk MSI rollout (suppress per-host)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's August-2024 Tickler write-up and the September-2023 Peach Sandstorm advisory both call out APT33's reliance on AnyDesk + Plink reverse-tunnels staged from user-writable paths after Entra ID account takeover — distinct from APT34/MuddyWater (POWGOOP / SimpleHelp) and APT35/Charming Kitten (custom .NET implants). The triple-correlation (RAT binary in user-writable path → outbound to AnyD
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
